html:
Fruit Medley is a Fruit Food Item cooked in a Crock Pot by combining 3.0 units of Fruits (except Dragon Fruit, which will result in Dragonpie). Unless a Twig is added, the recipe for Fruit Medley may result in Fist Full of Jam instead, as they share the same Crock Pot Priority. It takes 10 seconds to cook. In DST, RoG, and Shipwrecked, Fruit Medley decreases the perceived temperature by 1 degrees per second for 5 seconds.


Adding two or more Durians will result in Monster Lasagna unless a Twig is added. Adding Meats or Vegetables will result in Meatballs or Ratatouille respectively.

As Berries have a fruit value of 0.5, this recipe cannot include more than 2 Berries.

Being a Fruit Food Item, Wigfrid will not eat it.

Prerequisites

  • Requires:
  • Excludes:
  • Excludes:
  • Excludes:

Recipe

×3.0

Filler needs to be a Twig or may result in Fist Full of Jam

Examples

×3

×3

×2

×2

50% chance to result in Fist Full of Jam

×3

50% chance to result in Fist Full of Jam

×3

×3

50% chance of cooking Fist Full of Jam (See Bugs)

Usage

Tips

  • Since the individual hunger value of most compatible ingredients is higher than Fruit Medley itself, the use of a twig as filler is highly recommended to avoid accidentally making a Fist Full of Jam.
  • Despite requiring a higher fruit value than Fist Full of Jam, Fist Full of Jam restores more hunger, as such, Fruit Medleys should be made for healing and cooling purposes as opposed to food over Fist Fulls of Jam.

Bugs

  • Fruit Medley does not exclude Fishes.
    • Shipwrecked DLC introduces food items that have Fish value without any Meat value, such as Fish Morsel and Jellyfish. This causes using a Fish Morsel or Jellyfish in a recipe with fruit to produce a Fruit Medley despite technically containing meat.

Gallery

  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

Unless a Twig is added, the recipe for Fruit Medley may result in Fist Full of Jam instead, as they share the same Crock Pot Priority. It takes 10 seconds to cook. In DST, RoG, and Shipwrecked, Fruit Medley decreases the perceived temperature by 1 degrees per second for 5 seconds.
Adding two or more Durians will result in Monster Lasagna unless a Twig is added. Adding Meats or Vegetables will result in Meatballs or Ratatouille respectively.
As Berries have a fruit value of 0.5, this recipe cannot include more than 2 Berries.
Being a Fruit Food Item, Wigfrid will not eat it.

'''Fruit Medley''' is a [[Fruit]] [[Food]] [[Item]] cooked in a [[Crock Pot]] by combining 3.0 units of Fruits (except [[Dragon Fruit]], which will result in [[Dragonpie]]). Unless a [[Twigs|Twig]] is added, the recipe for Fruit Medley may result in [[Fist Full of Jam]] instead, as they share the same [[Crock Pot#Priority|Crock Pot Priority]]. It takes 10 seconds to cook.  In ''[[Don't Starve Together|DST]]'', ''[[Don't Starve: Reign of Giants|RoG]]'', and ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'', Fruit Medley decreases the perceived temperature by 1 degrees per second for 5 seconds.


Adding two or more [[Durian]]s will result in [[Monster Lasagna]] unless a Twig is added. Adding [[Meats]] or [[Vegetables]] will result in [[Meatballs]] or [[Ratatouille]] respectively.

As [[Berries]] have a fruit value of 0.5, this recipe cannot include more than 2 Berries.

Being a Fruit Food Item, [[Wigfrid]] will not eat it.

== Prerequisites ==
* Requires: [[File:Fruits Crock Pot(No Dragonfruit).png|link=Fruit]]
* Excludes: [[File:Dragon Fruits.png|link=Dragon Fruit]]
* Excludes: [[File:Meats Crock Pot.png|link=Meats]]
* Excludes: [[File:Vegetables Crock Pot.png|link=Vegetables]]

== {{pic|32|Cookbook}} Recipe ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Fruit
|count1 = 3.0
|item2 = Filler
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Fruit Medley
|note = Filler needs to be a [[Twigs|Twig]] or may result in [[Fist Full of Jam]]
|comparator1=×}}

== Examples ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Durian
|count1 = 3
|item2 = Twigs
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Fruit Medley
}}
{{Recipe
|item2 = Pomegranate
|count2 = 3
|item3 = Twigs
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Fruit Medley
}}
{{Recipe
|item1 = Pomegranate
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Fruit Medley
|note = 50% chance to result in [[Fist Full of Jam]]
|count1=2|count2=2|item2=Berries}}
{{Recipe
|item1 = Banana
|count1 = 3
|item2 = Ice
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Fruit Medley
|dlc = Reign of Giants
|note = 50% chance to result in [[Fist Full of Jam]]
}}
{{Recipe
|item1 = Halved Coconut
|count1 = 3
|item2 = Twigs
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Fruit Medley
|dlc = Shipwrecked
}}{{Recipe
|item1 = Banana SW
|count1 = 3
|item2 = Cooked Jellyfish
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Fruit Medley
|dlc = Shipwrecked
|note = 50% chance of cooking [[Fist Full of Jam]] (See Bugs)
}}
== {{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage ==
{{Recipe
|item = Fruit Medley
|tool = Pig Icon
|result = Manure
}}

=={{Pic|32|Prototype}} Tips==
* Since the individual hunger value of most compatible ingredients is higher than Fruit Medley itself, the use of a twig as filler is highly recommended to avoid accidentally making a Fist Full of Jam.
*Despite requiring a higher fruit value than Fist Full of Jam, Fist Full of Jam restores more hunger, as such, Fruit Medleys should be made for healing and cooling purposes as opposed to food over Fist Fulls of Jam.
== {{Pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs ==
* Fruit Medley does not exclude [[Fishes]].
**''[[Shipwrecked]]'' DLC introduces food items that have [[Fishes|Fish]] value without any [[Meats|Meat]] value, such as [[Fish Morsel]] and [[Jellyfish]]. This causes using a [[Fish Morsel]] or [[Jellyfish]] in a recipe with fruit to produce a Fruit Medley despite technically containing meat.
